text: A person has fallen overboard and is being picked up with a rescue boat. If the person appears in immediate danger of drowning, how should the rescue boat maneuver?

options.text: Perform an elegant, slow Williamson turn
Make the most direct and safest immediate approach
Circle the person three times before stopping

explanation: In cases of immediate danger, the rescue boat must prioritize the most direct and safest approach to extract the victim from the water as quickly as possible to prevent drowning.
